chunks.text: My father presented me a small box.
Inside were old photographs and one letter.
He had protected them for a long time.
In one photo, my young father smiled next to my grandfather.
" This is my childhood," he whispered softly.
I felt the ordinary box as something precious.
I had never imagined that a piece of paper could carry such worth.
Father taught me the bond and the affection of family, not with words, but quietly.
" I give you this," and the small occasion carried the respect and honor of long years.
I nodded to my father and accepted the box.
A gentle wish to give something back grew in my heart.
